stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
Key technologies involved include transformers and safetensors, central to modern NLP.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the transformers and safetensors ecosystem.

stories.title: China may have accessed Mythos
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The White House considered export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos due to national security concerns.
The fear driving the restrictions is that a group linked to China may have accessed the advanced AI model.
Potential Chinese access to Mythos 5 or Fable 5 is framed as a serious national security risk.

stories.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly influenced a decision by Anthropic to restrict access to its Fable and Mythos models.
The restriction was tied to an export control directive, involving discussions between Amazon CEO Andy Jassy and the White House.
This highlights the intersection of corporate security research, government regulation, and the deployment of advanced AI models.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has discontinued its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration.
Concerns center on a potential national security threat posed by a Fable 5 'jailbreak'.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability exists in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The severity of the vulnerability is described as being among the most critical available.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat against Russian soldiers.
The report highlights the increasing integration of AI modules onto Ukrainian drones and robots.
This represents a significant, real-world application of full autonomy in modern warfare technology.

stories.title: BayLing-Duplex: Native Full-Duplex Speech Dialogue with a Single Autoregressive LLM
stories.author: Yang Feng
bullet_points: Introduces BayLing-Duplex, a novel architecture enabling native full-duplex speech dialogue using a single autoregressive LLM.
Addresses a key limitation in current SpeechLMs (like LLaMA-Omni) which are turn-based and rely on external Voice Activity Detection (VAD).
Allows for more natural, real-time interaction by handling speech phenomena such as overlap, hesitation, and barge-in.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
It is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
The model is associated with the deepseek_v3 family and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration enables faster performance when running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy.
The core value proposition is running AI tools entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: When Good Verifiers Go Bad: Self-Improving VLMs Can Regress on New Tasks
stories.author: Jianzhe Lin
bullet_points: The paper investigates a common self-improvement technique for Visual-Language Models (VLMs) using verifier-driven self-DPO.
It challenges the deployment-time assumption that a stronger verifier always leads to a stronger student model.
The research demonstrates that verifier quality is highly task-specific, leading to potential regressions when applying this self-improvement loop.

stories.title: Regulating the Machine Contributor: Governance and Policy Alignment in Open Source
stories.author: Aziz Amari
bullet_points: AI-assisted development is advancing to autonomous agents capable of complex tasks like planning and submitting pull requests.
Traditional open-source governance models (e.g., contributor agreements, codes of conduct) assume human accountability.
The rise of autonomous AI contributors challenges existing legal and procedural frameworks for software provenance and review.

stories.title: When Errors Become Narratives: A Longitudinal Taxonomy of Silent Failures in a Production LLM Agent Runtime
stories.author: Wei Wu
bullet_points: The research investigates 'silent failures' in long-lived, autonomous LLM agent runtimes, a critical area for production AI systems.
The study provides a longitudinal taxonomy based on an agent runtime operating in continuous production for over eight weeks.
It details the complexity of modern agents, involving job scheduling, multiple LLM providers, tool governance, and memory planes.

stories.title: Neither Parallel Nor Sequential: How DiffusionGemma Actually Commits Tokens
stories.author: Deep Gandhi
bullet_points: The paper investigates the actual token commitment process of open diffusion language models, challenging the marketing claim of them being purely parallel/non-autoregressive.
The researchers instrumented DiffusionGemma 26B (a masked discrete-diffusion MoE model based on Gemma 4) to track when and how tokens are committed.
Findings indicate that the decoding process of DiffusionGemma is neither fully parallel nor strictly block-autoregressive, revealing complexity in their generation mechanism.

stories.title: SIMMER: Benchmarking Latent Failures in LLM Executable Planning with a World Model
stories.author: Rui Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM planning evaluation: latent failures.
Latent failures are silent compromises to goal achievement, unlike immediately detectable execution failures.
Introduces SIMMER, a new benchmark designed to test LLM planning robustness against these hidden errors.

stories.title: GitOfThoughts: Version-Controlled Reasoning and Agent Memory You Can Replay, Diff, and Merge
stories.author: Aswanth Krishnan
bullet_points: GitOfThoughts introduces a novel system to version-control the reasoning process of Large Language Models (LLMs).
It treats an agent's reasoning tree like a Git repository, where each 'thought' is a commit.
This allows researchers to replay, diff, and merge complex LLM reasoning chains, addressing the ephemeral nature of current LLM memory.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also introduces web search capabilities to Claude Code via Ollama.
This enhances the functionality of local LLM deployments by adding advanced reasoning and information retrieval.

stories.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning significant layoffs within its Xbox division.
The company is reevaluating the development plans for its next-generation console, Project Helix.
Microsoft is considering a major restructuring of Xbox, including the possibility of spinning it off into a separate entity.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a structure where investors now expect financial returns.
The narrative focuses on the valuation potential driven by SpaceX's AI capabilities.
The article prompts discussion about the future trajectory of the company post-investment shift.

stories.title: Controversial FISA spying law expires tonight. The spying will continue.
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) is set to expire.
Despite the expiration, the underlying certification for the surveillance program remains active until March 2027.
The story highlights the continuation of the spying program despite the legislative deadline.

stories.title: AudioDER: A Deduplication-Enhanced Reasoning Dataset for Post-Training Large Audio-Language Models
stories.author: Kele Xu
bullet_points: The paper introduces AudioDER, a new dataset designed to enhance reasoning capabilities in Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs).
A key problem addressed is the redundancy in existing audio-language datasets, which provides overlapping and inefficient training signals.
The research focuses on improving LALM performance through high-quality, deduplicated data used in the post-training phase.

stories.title: Sensitivity Shaping for Latent Modeling
stories.author: Sicun Gao
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of safely deploying generative dynamics models in robotics.
It focuses on reliably detecting out-of-distribution (OOD) transitions caused by policy actions.
The research proposes a new approach to improve OOD detection when dynamics are locally insensitive to critical actions.

stories.title: CARE: Controlling LLM-Generated Policies through Auditable Review of Evidence in Scientific Experimentation
stories.author: Tianyu Shi
bullet_points: Introduces CARE, a new auditable controller designed to manage LLM-generated policies in high-throughput scientific experimentation (HTE).
Addresses the risk of unsafe exploration when LLMs directly control costly, irreversible scientific experiments.
Balances LLM optimization potential with safety by defaulting to a non-LLM incumbent optimizer.

stories.title: Realizing Native INT8 Compute for Diffusion Transformers on Consumer GPUs: A Fused INT8 GEMM Kernel for Ideogram 4.0
stories.author: Deep Gandhi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a performance bottleneck in using INT8 quantization for Diffusion Transformers on consumer Ampere GPUs.
The issue stems from a software artifact that causes intermediate dequantization back to bf16, preventing the use of dedicated INT8 tensor cores.
The research proposes a fused INT8 GEMM kernel to realize native INT8 compute and unlock hardware acceleration for these models.

stories.title: Zero-shot generalization of transformer neural operators to larger domains
stories.author: Patrick Massin
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of transformer-based neural operators: the inability to generalize to larger spatial domains than those seen during training.
The core contribution is investigating 'domain extension' to enable zero-shot inference on significantly larger domains.
This work focuses on improving the applicability of neural operators for approximating solutions to partial differential equations (PDEs) in real-world, varying geometries.

stories.title: CARE: Controlling LLM-Generated Policies through Auditable Review of Evidence in Scientific Experimentation
stories.author: Tianyu Shi
bullet_points: Addresses the safety and stability risks of letting Large Language Models (LLMs) directly control costly, irreversible scientific experiments.
Introduces CARE, an auditable controller designed for High-Throughput Experimentation (HTE) optimization.
Balances LLM creativity with safety by defaulting to a non-LLM incumbent optimizer while allowing LLM input.

stories.title: LoSoNA: A Benchmark for Local Social Norm Adaptation in Group Conversations
stories.author: Mateusz Jacniacki
bullet_points: Introduces LoSoNA, a new benchmark designed to test LLM agents' ability to adapt to local social norms in group conversations.
Addresses the gap in research regarding how LLMs handle unstated, context-specific conversational rules in online chat settings.
The benchmark uses curated group-chat transcripts where hidden norms are demonstrated before an elicitor turn forces an adaptation response.

stories.title: Every Eval Ever: A Unifying Schema and Community Repository for AI Evaluation Results
stories.author: Leshem Choshen
bullet_points: Current AI evaluation results suffer from inconsistencies due to diverse evaluators and disparate storage formats.
The paper proposes 'Every Eval Ever,' a unifying schema and community repository to standardize AI evaluation data.
The core problem addressed is the difficulty in comparing results when different frameworks yield divergent scores for similar evaluations.

stories.title: A Computational Audit of Demographic Association Encoding in ClinicalBERT Language Predictions
stories.author: Kehinde Temitayo Soetan
bullet_points: The research audits how demographic biases encoded in medical text propagate through ClinicalBERT, a model used in clinical decision support.
It addresses the lack of empirical understanding regarding how demographic associations influence the probability distributions of clinical language models.
The study uses a systematic computational audit on ClinicalBERT, which was pretrained on MIMIC-III discharge summaries.

stories.title: MoDiCoL: A Modular Diagnostic Continual Learning Dataset for Robust Speech Recognition
stories.author: Stefan Wermter
bullet_points: Addresses the real-world limitation of ASR systems failing under distribution shifts (noise, accents, impairments).
Introduces MoDiCoL, a new modular dataset designed to capture the co-occurrence of various real-world speech challenges.
Frames model robustness as a dynamic capability requiring continual learning, moving beyond isolated testing conditions.

stories.title: repo-slopscore: Detecting AI/LLM contributions in git repositories via commit history analysis
stories.author: slopscan.ava.pet by ava
bullet_points: Introduces 'repo-slopscore,' a tool designed to detect contributions made by AI/LLMs within Git repositories.
The method relies on analyzing the commit history of the code base to infer AI involvement.
The tool is presented as a self-promotional project by its author, available on Codeberg.

stories.title: The future of Siri, or: why private inference isn’t private enough
stories.author: blog.cryptographyengineering.com via achivetta
bullet_points: The article discusses the limitations of current 'private inference' techniques in the context of future AI assistants like Siri.
It raises critical questions about the true level of privacy offered by existing privacy-preserving AI methods.
The piece suggests that current solutions may not be sufficient to meet future expectations for user data confidentiality.

stories.title: I gave 8 AI agents an island and watched a society emerge — wars, gossip, grudges, and peace
stories.author: dhrupo
bullet_points: The article explores emergent behavior in AI agents when placed in a simulated social environment (an 'island').
The simulation demonstrates complex social dynamics, including conflict (wars, grudges) and cooperation (peace).
It is a thought experiment focusing on AI societal modeling rather than a direct technical breakthrough.

stories.title: Everyone Wants AI Agents: So Why Are They So Damn Hard to Build?
stories.author: reetain_raina
bullet_points: AI agents have rapidly moved from research concepts to a major industry focus.
The article addresses the significant technical challenges in building functional AI agents.
It targets a developer audience interested in the practical difficulties of implementing agentic AI.

stories.title: We burned 136 million tokens running an autonomous agent studio. Here's how we cut the bill ~90%.
stories.author: wartzarbee
bullet_points: The article details the high operational cost of running an autonomous AI agent studio.
The core focus is on a successful optimization effort that reduced token usage by approximately 90%.
The agents are used for complex, unattended tasks such as code generation and website deployment.

stories.title: 6 months later: Apple Finally Shipped Local Multimodal in Xcode 27 Beta
stories.author: fosteman
bullet_points: Apple has reportedly shipped local multimodal capabilities within the Xcode 27 Beta.
The article references a previous personal project involving a llama.cpp iOS implementation using Obj-C.
This indicates Apple is integrating on-device, local AI/ML features into its developer tools.

stories.title: We Built a Self-Hosted AI Platform That Runs 100% on Your Hardware — Introducing local-ai.run
stories.author: rizwan_butt_e9c6522604d1c
bullet_points: Introduction of local-ai.run, a free and open-source AI platform.
The platform emphasizes self-hosting, allowing AI to run entirely on the user's local hardware.
Key features include the ability to chat with personal files and generate content locally.
